children.text: When the terms “employer”, “employee”, and “wage” are used in this chapter in relation to the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938, as amended [

children.tail: et seq.], they shall have the same meaning as when used in such Act of 1938.

children.text: When the term “employer” is used in this chapter in relation to the Walsh-Healey Act or Bacon-Davis Act

children.tail: See References in Text note below.
it shall mean the contractor or subcontractor covered by such Act.

children.text: When the term “employee” is used in this chapter in relation to the Walsh-Healey Act or the Bacon-Davis Act

children.tail: it shall mean any individual employed by the contractor or subcontractor covered by such Act in the performance of his contract or subcontract.
So in original. Probably should be “Walsh-Healey Act”.
means the Act entitled “An Act to provide conditions for the purchase of supplies and the making of contracts by the United States, and for other purposes”, approved
and the term “Bacon-Davis Act” means the Act entitled “An Act to amend the Act approved
, relating to the rate of wages for laborers and mechanics employed by contractors and subcontractors on public buildings”, approved
the term “State” means any State of the United States or the District of Columbia or any Territory or possession of the United States.

children.text: The “Bacon-Davis Act”, as defined for purposes of this chapter in subsec. (d), is

children.tail: , popularly known as the “Davis-Bacon Act”, and which was classified to sections 276a to 276a–6 of former Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works.
